description_long: This object is a classic revolver-style firearm with a long, octagonal barrel and a six-chambered rotating cylinder. The frame and barrel are metallic, showing a worn, matte steel finish, while the grip is made of polished dark wood with a curved ergonomic shape. The hammer is exposed and cocked back, and the trigger is housed within a rounded metal guard. The cylinder features visible fluting and is integrated into the main body, with a loading lever beneath the barrel. The overall design is reminiscent of 19th-century Colt revolvers, with a robust and utilitarian appearance. The object is detailed with metallic textures and subtle color variations, giving it a realistic, aged look.
description: A classic revolver-style firearm with a long steel barrel, rotating cylinder, and polished wooden grip, featuring a worn metallic finish and exposed hammer.
description_view_0: The object is viewed directly from the rear, showing the rounded wooden grip and the back of the metallic frame.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the long barrel, fluted cylinder, exposed hammer, and the full profile of the wooden grip.
description_view_2: The object is seen from the front, focusing on the barrel's muzzle and the circular opening of the cylinder.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the barrel, cylinder, trigger guard, and the opposite side of the wooden grip.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel, long octagonal barrel with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, medium-sized metal guard with smooth edges and no finger grooves
